title: RuleVerdict
description: The deterministic verdict produced by one CQL rule library in the A2 rule layer (verification/rules/engine.js), after executing the compiled ELM against packet-derived parameters. Folded into the verification result additively + monotone by composeRules() — it can add a review flag or a caveat, never rescue or downgrade, never flip pass, and never emit a dose. Rides the audit channel; never merged into the ContextPacket. Mirrors the zod RuleVerdictSchema (.strict()) in engine.js.

rule_id.description: Stable id of the rule (matches the library basename, e.g. 'paediatric-review').

version.description: The rule version, read from the compiled ELM's library.identifier.version — so a verdict can never claim a version the artifact does not carry.

outcome.description: proceed = no action; review = route to in-person review (a CAUTION/routing signal, NOT a verification failure); blocked_incomplete = a required input was missing to decide.

flags.description: Machine flags added to verification (e.g. 'in_person_review_required'). Additive only.

caveats.description: Non-blocking caveats surfaced to the clinician (e.g. 'plausible_gillick_competence_expected'). A human judgment the rule does not compute.

evidence.description: What produced the verdict — the ELM library and the parameters it was given. Audit/reproducibility.
